content: solders 0.27.1 has NO SPL Token instruction builders (solders.token only exposes get_associated_token_address + Mint/TokenAccount/Multisig state decoders) -- close_account must come from elsewhere. The 'solana' PyPI package (solana-py, michaelhly/solana-py, currently 0.40.0) provides spl.token.instructions.close_account(CloseAccountParams(account, dest, owner, program_id)) but adds a full RPC-wrapping dependency this project's own CLAUDE.md explicitly discourages for the fund-moving path (audit-surface minimization). CloseAccount account metas (per solana-py source): [account(writable,not signer), dest(writable,not signer), owner(signer,not writable)]. TokenInstruction::CloseAccount is enum variant 9 (single-byte discriminant, no further instruction data) per the spl-token Rust program. TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID = TokenkegQfeZyiNwAJbNbGKPFXCWuBvf9Ss623VQ5DA. Hand-encoding via solders.instruction.Instruction(TOKEN_PROGRAM_ID, bytes([9]), [AccountMeta(...)]) was verified to construct successfully against the installed solders package.
